entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A nutritious food source and important livelihood option for subsistence farming communities.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The largest chickpea producing nation where the study was conducted.

entities.name: YIELD GAP ANALYSIS
entities.description: A modelling approach used to quantify region-specific constraints and yield gaps limiting productivity.

entities.name: YIELD GAP
entities.description: The difference between the yield potential and the currently achieved yields.

entities.name: CHICKPEA PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The output of chickpea production in various regions.

entities.name: SSM-ILEGUME MODEL
entities.description: A model used to reproduce seasonal variability and potential yield for major chickpea producing districts.

entities.name: POTENTIAL YIELD
entities.description: The maximum achievable yield under standard crop management practices.

entities.name: RAIN-FED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Cropping systems where production is largely limited by water availability.

entities.name: WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The availability of water during the growing season, a primary limiting factor in rain-fed systems.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: The effect of water scarcity on chickpea yields.

entities.name: HOMOGENEOUS SYSTEM UNITS
entities.description: Clusters of chickpea-growing districts with similar bio-geo-physical properties and system responses.

entities.name: GENOTYPE-BY-MANAGEMENT INTERVENTION
entities.description: Interventions involving the interaction between genotype and management practices.

entities.name: BREEDING UNITS
entities.description: Authentic units proposed for crop improvement programs based on HSUs.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at enhancing crop production through breeding.

entities.name: HSU-SPECIFIC BREEDING STRATEGY
entities.description: A breeding strategy tailored to specific homogeneous system units to enhance production.
